3:
Bloody hells... Flooding their own castle for a better chance at victory...
We could learn a thing or two from that lad.

Bloody hells... Flooding their own castle for a better chance at victory...
We could learn a thing or two from that lad.

6:
We have a chance, aye─mayhap the last one we're ever gonna get.
While we've done our best to rebuild the Resistance, the imperials have only increased their efforts to root out sympathizers.

We have a chance, aye─mayhap the last one we're ever gonna get.
While we've done our best to rebuild the Resistance, the imperials have only increased their efforts to root out sympathizers.

9:
Then let us waste no more time.
The campaign for Ala Mhigan liberation begins now!

Then let us waste no more time.
The campaign for Ala Mhigan liberation begins now!

10:
Our first objective will be to capture Castellum Velodyna. Take the bridge,
and the whole of the Fringes will be ours. From there, we can push east
towards the Peaks.

Our first objective will be to capture Castellum Velodyna. Take the bridge,
and the whole of the Fringes will be ours. From there, we can push east
towards the Peaks.

34:
Idiot! We have the stronger force by far! Don't be taken in by their tricks!
Or would you rather tell Zenos you lost to this rabble!?

Idiot! We have the stronger force by far! Don't be taken in by their tricks!
Or would you rather tell Zenos you lost to this rabble!?

35:
No, but if the snakes betray us in the confusion, we'll have no way out!
I'm begging you, Commander─give the order, or we'll all die here!

No, but if the snakes betray us in the confusion, we'll have no way out!
I'm begging you, Commander─give the order, or we'll all die here!

37:
Skulls, with me! The day is theirs, and so is this bastard bridge.
Someone bring the snake!

Skulls, with me! The day is theirs, and so is this bastard bridge.
Someone bring the snake!

39:
Let them go, Lyse! We've done what we came here to do.
That's enough for now.

Let them go, Lyse! We've done what we came here to do.
That's enough for now.

43:
Good, all things considered. Better than the Ananta, at any rate...
They've sent an emissary. I'll take you to her.

Good, all things considered. Better than the Ananta, at any rate...
They've sent an emissary. I'll take you to her.

49:
But then a new commander was sssent to hold the black bridge.
She demanded the Qalyana sssurrender a hossstage, thisss Butcher.

But then a new commander was sssent to hold the black bridge.
She demanded the Qalyana sssurrender a hossstage, thisss Butcher.

52:
I see. All Fordola knew for sure was that the Resistance would come from
the west. She reasoned, therefore, that if the Ananta on the east bank turned their coats, she would be trapped. I take it the Qalyana had no choice but
to oblige her.

I see. All Fordola knew for sure was that the Resistance would come from
the west. She reasoned, therefore, that if the Ananta on the east bank turned their coats, she would be trapped. I take it the Qalyana had no choice but
to oblige her.

62:
The true madnesss was yet to come. For in her dessspair, the Qalyana broodmother cried out for her daughter to be ressstored to life.
She bessseeched Sri Lakshmi's intercession.

The true madnesss was yet to come. For in her dessspair, the Qalyana broodmother cried out for her daughter to be ressstored to life.
She bessseeched Sri Lakshmi's intercession.

64:
That she did, if only for an inssstant. Bereft of courage and honor,
the broodmother sssought sssolace in her faith.

That she did, if only for an inssstant. Bereft of courage and honor,
the broodmother sssought sssolace in her faith.

66:
Now the Qalyana bid usss make pilgrimage, to pay proper ressspectsss.
Yet though we Vira revere Sri Lakshmi as the holiessst of the holy, we will not prossstrate ourssselves before Her.

Now the Qalyana bid usss make pilgrimage, to pay proper ressspectsss.
Yet though we Vira revere Sri Lakshmi as the holiessst of the holy, we will not prossstrate ourssselves before Her.

69:
I believe we have heard enough. Inconvenient though the timing may be, if a primal has indeed been summoned, we can scarce afford to ignore it.
We must needs discuss how best to resolve this situation.

I believe we have heard enough. Inconvenient though the timing may be, if a primal has indeed been summoned, we can scarce afford to ignore it.
We must needs discuss how best to resolve this situation.

73:
But she hears not her mother's words, nor sssees her mother's face!
She...she is sssilent...and empty...

But she hears not her mother's words, nor sssees her mother's face!
She...she is sssilent...and empty...

74:
Only the vesssel may be remade. Not so the flame which flickered within.
Her soul hath been scattered to the four winds.

Only the vesssel may be remade. Not so the flame which flickered within.
Her soul hath been scattered to the four winds.

80:
I understand how you feel, I do. You loved her with all your heart, but when the moment came, you still couldn't save her. The anguish, the anger...
I know it only too well.

I understand how you feel, I do. You loved her with all your heart, but when the moment came, you still couldn't save her. The anguish, the anger...
I know it only too well.

93:
So...it looks like the Ananta did have a mountain of crystals after all.
Like every other tribe. Gods, we're all such slaves to our weaknesses...

So...it looks like the Ananta did have a mountain of crystals after all.
Like every other tribe. Gods, we're all such slaves to our weaknesses...

94:
You! You ssslew our Missstresss! She was our hope, our sssalvation!
Is there no end to your cruelty!?

You! You ssslew our Missstresss! She was our hope, our sssalvation!
Is there no end to your cruelty!?

105:
(-Imperial Tribunus Militum-)Ah, the savage returns. And with her tail firmly between her legs.
Beaten by beastmen, I hear.

(-Imperial Tribunus Militum-)Ah, the savage returns. And with her tail firmly between her legs.
Beaten by beastmen, I hear.

115:
You were defeated by the Resistance on several occasions.
You misjudged the Ananta and spurred them into summoning an eikon.
Have you anything to say?

You were defeated by the Resistance on several occasions.
You misjudged the Ananta and spurred them into summoning an eikon.
Have you anything to say?

116:
I do not, my lord. My failures are my own.
I am prepared to accept your judgment.

I do not, my lord. My failures are my own.
I am prepared to accept your judgment.

124:
I remember how they laughed. Alliance and Resistance soldiers,
eager for battle, trading jokes as we marched east towards the Peaks...

I remember how they laughed. Alliance and Resistance soldiers,
eager for battle, trading jokes as we marched east towards the Peaks...

130:
So...she lives. Impressive...or merely lucky.
Though that too may prove a useful trait.

So...she lives. Impressive...or merely lucky.
Though that too may prove a useful trait.

136:
Through modification of the Garlean genome, I argued that it might be possible to enhance a subject's ability to manipulate aether, effectively empowering them to wield magicks. Alas, my theories were met with consternation in the imperial court.
Only Lord Zenos, with commendable foresight and wisdom, deigned to support my research.

Through modification of the Garlean genome, I argued that it might be possible to enhance a subject's ability to manipulate aether, effectively empowering them to wield magicks. Alas, my theories were met with consternation in the imperial court.
Only Lord Zenos, with commendable foresight and wisdom, deigned to support my research.

143:
No. Even were you to leave now, you would not arrive in time.
There is another duty I would entrust to you...

No. Even were you to leave now, you would not arrive in time.
There is another duty I would entrust to you...

152:
A unit of legionaries has been mustered and is ready to depart.
They await your orders, Commander.

A unit of legionaries has been mustered and is ready to depart.
They await your orders, Commander.

156:
Commander, if I may! Our people─your people are still in there!
They will be slaughtered!

Commander, if I may! Our people─your people are still in there!
They will be slaughtered!

162:
Wars are won on the backs of the dead. Theirs and ours. There is no truth but this. We must remain firm and resolute, and always, always do our duty.
Now, give the order!

Wars are won on the backs of the dead. Theirs and ours. There is no truth but this. We must remain firm and resolute, and always, always do our duty.
Now, give the order!

164:
Aye, aye, initiating firing sequence!
Release safety locks...deploy main cannon!

Aye, aye, initiating firing sequence!
Release safety locks...deploy main cannon!

165:
Ansfrid, Hrudolf, Emelin...
For us. For our people. For our future.

Ansfrid, Hrudolf, Emelin...
For us. For our people. For our future.

170:
Those treacherous bastards... Their people were still fighting!
Full retreat, now! Relay the orders!

Those treacherous bastards... Their people were still fighting!
Full retreat, now! Relay the orders!

195:
Notify all units─I want that intruder found!
And get the engineers to fix my bloody cannon!

Notify all units─I want that intruder found!
And get the engineers to fix my bloody cannon!

198:
Ah...I think that answers my question.
We have accomplished our objectives as well.

Ah...I think that answers my question.
We have accomplished our objectives as well.

206:
It was over! The Skulls had surrendered! No one else had to die!
And you killed them! Your own people! Your own comrades!

It was over! The Skulls had surrendered! No one else had to die!
And you killed them! Your own people! Your own comrades!

215:
Shut up! Shut your mouth! You don't get to talk about freedom!
You killed your own! They trusted you and you killed them!

Shut up! Shut your mouth! You don't get to talk about freedom!
You killed your own! They trusted you and you killed them!

231:
Same could be said of the village. It was damn near impossible to live off
the land, barren as it was─which is why so many of us traded our plowshares for swords.

the land, barren as it was─which is why so many of us traded our plowshares for swords.

246:
Conrad chose you as his successor, Lyse, and I will not gainsay his decision.
I have but one question to ask.

Conrad chose you as his successor, Lyse, and I will not gainsay his decision.
I have but one question to ask.

249:
I swear to you, here and now:
I will fight until the end, be it liberty or be it death.

I swear to you, here and now:
I will fight until the end, be it liberty or be it death.

252:
Hmm. A good oath. Then there is naught else to say.
You are indeed your father's daughter...
